title: About
text:
I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the National Institute of Technology Warangal (NIT Warangal), India.
My research lies at the intersection of Scientific Machine Learning, Applied Mathematics, and Control Theory, where I develop mathematically rigorous learning algorithms for scientific and engineering applications.
My research combines mathematical analysis, numerical methods, optimization, and machine learning to solve challenging problems arising in differential equations, dynamical systems, scientific computing, and control engineering. A central theme of my work is integrating modern artificial intelligence with classical mathematical theory to develop reliable, interpretable, and theoretically guaranteed computational methods.
Prior to joining NIT Warangal, I worked as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Laboratoire de Mécanique Paris-Saclay, École Normale Supérieure Paris-Saclay, Université Paris-Saclay, France, under the supervision of Prof. Ludovic Chamoin, where my research focused on Learning-Based Model Predictive Control, Neural Networks, and Scientific Machine Learning.

title: Can quantum-mechanical description of physical reality be considered complete?
text:
Abstract
In a complete theory there is an element corresponding to each element of reality. A sufficient condition for the reality of a physical quantity is the possibility of predicting it with certainty, without disturbing the system. In quantum mechanics in the case of two physical quantities described by non-commuting operators, the knowledge of one precludes the knowledge of the other. Then either (1) the description of reality given by the wave function in quantum mechanics is not complete or (2) these two quantities cannot have simultaneous reality. Consideration of the problem of making predictions concerning a system on the basis of measurements made on another system that had previously interacted with it leads to the result that if (1) is false then (2) is also false. One is thus led to conclude that the description of reality as given by a wave function is not complete.

title: Can quantum-mechanical description of physical reality be considered complete?

title: Projects
text:
My current long-term research directions include

Scientific Machine Learning for Engineering Systems
Neural Operators for Partial Differential Equations
Learning-Based Robust Model Predictive Control
AI for Scientific Computing
Fractional Dynamical Systems
Digital Twins for Structural Health Monitoring
Mathematical Foundations of Neural Differential Equations
Safe and Explainable Scientific Artificial Intelligence
